structured.title: Calls, Gala, and Fleet Plans
structured.overview: The conversation revolves around coordinating a risky social infiltration tied to a Gold Bank executive, a charity gala, and the Crimson Fleet. The speaker explains that to get what they need, they must attend the Terra Preservation Society gala, pose as a philanthropist, and gather dirt on a VIP executive. There’s also tension about motives, with one person insisting they want back into the Crimson Fleet rather than money. The discussion then shifts to ship access and security, including being a guest aboard a captain’s ship and restrictions on where they can go. The transcript ends with a separate frustration-driven tech workaround: a script in Jupyter is set up to repeatedly call back an annoying number and play Rick Astley on each answer, looping until the caller blocks the number.

apps_results.content:
## Initial confrontation
- Asked what time you arrived
- Demanded to know what’s going on
- Mentioned three years of silence

## Mission and suspicion
- Delgado ordered help after a big score
- Asked if the message was only about Demgost
- Suspected theft, not just cooperation

## Deal and gala plan
- User wants back into Crimson Fleet
- Refused money; demanded Fleet reentry
- Need to attend the charity gala
- Use a card to access the ship

## Security and risks
- Target is a GalBank executive
- Gala is packed with wealthy attendees
- Ship has acoustic threat detection
- Avoid the gala days

## Ship access and restrictions
- Captain Rolkov allows limited access
- Told not to touch anything
- Ship has heavy security upgrades

## Harassment workaround
- Created a script to auto-call them
- It answers prompts and plays Rick Astley
- Loop runs forever until blocked
